cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking for the date one week from the last day of 1899. The last day of 1899 would be December 31, 1899, right? So if we add one week to that date, which is seven days, we need to figure out what the new date would be. First, I need to remember how the calendar works for the end of the year. If today is December 31, then adding one day would take us to January 1 of the next year. So if we add seven days, we need to count each day carefully. Let me count day by day: - December 31, 1899 (starting point) - January 1, 1900 (1 day later) - January 2, 1900 (2 days) - January 3, 1900 (3 days) - January 4, 1900 (4 days) - January 5, 1900 (5 days) - January 6, 1900 (6 days) - January 7, 1900 (7 days) So one week from December 31, 1899, would be January 7, 1900. Now I need to check the options given. Looking at the options: (A) 02/07/1900 (B) 02/22/1900 (C) 01/13/1900 (D) 01/07/1900 (E) 10/07/1900 (F) 01/07/1828 The correct date is January 7, 1900, so the correct answer is (D) 01/07/1900.
